WI.hero_title: Wisconsin: Where ThedaCare Leads and Froedtert Lags
WI.hero_subtitle: The state with the highest median transparency â€” and the sharpest system-level contrasts.
WI.intro_text: ThedaCare achieves 100% transparency at all 9 facilities. Froedtert Hospital â€” Milwaukee's flagship â€” discloses rates for only 6 payers. UW Health discloses 112 payer contracts but remains on the outdated v2.0.0 schema.
findings: ThedaCare is the transparency gold standard: 100% payer rate disclosure at all 9 facilities, up to 59 payers, current v3.0.0 schema, updated March 2026.
Froedtert Hospital â€” Milwaukee's largest, Level I Trauma Center â€” discloses rates for only 6 payers. A 25-bed critical access hospital in rural Boscobel (Gundersen) discloses more.

UW Health discloses the most payer contracts in the state (112) using percentage-of-gross methodology, but its file is stale (January 2025) and still on v2.0.0 â€” at risk for CMS enforcement beginning April 2026.
Ascension's three Wisconsin facilities are the least transparent (27â€“38%), consistent with national reporting on Ascension's disclosure approach.
Marshfield's 5% cash discount is effectively no discount â€” a patient saves $50 on a $1,000 procedure. The lowest meaningful discount in the state.
Gundersen's intra-system cash discount variance spans 21% to 62% across seven facilities â€” evidence of facility-level pricing autonomy.

MN.hero_subtitle: A consumer-governed nonprofit that hides what insurance actually pays.
MN.intro_text: HealthPartners â€” both an insurer and a hospital system â€” scores 6.9% at Regions Hospital. They give patients generous cash discounts (53â€“75% off gross) while hiding what insured patients' carriers actually negotiate.
findings: HealthPartners Regions Hospital â€” Level I Trauma, 454 beds, consumer-governed nonprofit â€” discloses payer rates on 6.9% of rows. The lowest non-zero score in the three-state dataset.
All four HealthPartners facilities score below 12% â€” the only system in either state entirely below 20%. Yet their cash discounts are the highest in Minnesota (53â€“75%).
Mayo Clinic Rochester discloses rates for 1,102 payer/plan combinations â€” 10x more than any other hospital in any state. The richest single chargemaster file in the dataset.
Allina Health achieves 99.8â€“100% across all 5 analyzed facilities â€” proving full disclosure is operationally feasible for a large multi-hospital system.
Essentia Brainerd (St. Joseph's Medical Center) scores 99.6% with 39 payers â€” one of the most transparent hospitals in the dataset and the strongest local data anchor.
M Health Fairview shows good percentage transparency (70â€“90%) but only 2â€“4 payers per facility â€” the structure has rates but for very few contracts.

IL.hero_subtitle: Northwestern and Rush comply fully. UChicago publishes almost nothing.
IL.intro_text: Three of Chicago's four academic flagships comply comprehensively. UChicago Medical Center discloses payer rates for only 2.9% of items â€” exclusively DRG bundles â€” while individual line items show only list prices.
findings: UChicago Medical Center discloses payer rates for 2.9% of items (2,302 of 78,504). Payer data exists only on DRG bundles; 97% of items show only gross charges and cash prices. Strategic selective disclosure.
Northwestern Memorial Hospital achieves ~95% transparency with 32 payers across v3.0.0 JSON â€” comprehensive disclosure from Chicago's largest hospital.
Rush University Medical Center hits 100% with 35 payers â€” proving Chicago's academic centers can comply fully.
Advocate Health is remarkably uniform: all 11 facilities cluster at 60â€“70% with exactly 18 payers and a 50% cash discount. Centralized but incomplete.
HSHS's Illinois hospitals (100%, 30â€“34 payers) outperform Ascension's Illinois presence (32â€“67%) â€” smaller system, better disclosure.
Loyola Gottlieb offers an 81% cash discount â€” the highest in the three-state dataset â€” while disclosing only 56% of payer rates.

IA.hero_title: Iowa: MercyOne Discloses Everything, UnityPoint Holds Back
IA.hero_subtitle: Two systems, two transparency philosophies — and 755 payers that prove full disclosure is possible.
IA.intro_text: MercyOne achieves 94–99% transparency across 13 Iowa facilities with 755+ payer contracts each — the most comprehensive disclosure in the Midwest. UnityPoint Health, Iowa's other major system, clusters at 51–75% with a uniform 20% cash discount. The gap is not technical — it's strategic.
findings: MercyOne is Iowa's transparency leader: 13 facilities at 94–99.5% transparency, each disclosing rates for 752–760 payers. No other Midwest system comes close to this payer breadth.
UnityPoint Health (14 facilities) clusters in the Substantial tier at 51–75% — all on v3.0.0 schema with a uniform 20% cash discount. Centralized pricing, incomplete disclosure.
University of Iowa Health Care hits 100% across both campuses — the state's flagship academic center complies fully.
Iowa Specialty Hospital (Clarion + Belmond) both achieve 100% with 12–15 payers and a 40% cash discount — proof that small hospitals can fully comply.
MercyOne's cash discount variance spans 9–36% across facilities — compared to UnityPoint's uniform 20%. MercyOne appears to set pricing at the facility level; UnityPoint centralizes it.
14 hospitals scored 0% — mostly non-Iowa facilities from national cms-hpt.txt files (Gundersen, Trinity, Genesis) that were incorrectly attributed to Iowa in discovery.
